title: Sky Lantern Migration Physics Lab
description: A physics sandbox where the user manages a flock of storks carrying lanterns across a pond, adjusting mass, buoyancy, and wind forces to guide them safely. A duck floats on the pond below acting as a movable target checkpoint. The user must balance momentum conservation, gravitational potential energy, and drag forces across multiple coupled inputs while using undo/redo to refine trajectories.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing a physics simulation lab. Load Lantern.glb, stork.glb, and Duck.glb using GLTFLoader. Scene setup: a flat blue semi-transparent plane as water at y=0, a subtle grid ground below, hemisphere light plus a warm directional light casting shadows. Camera starts at position (0,8,18) looking at origin with OrbitControls. Place 3 storks at varying heights (y=4 to y=8) across x positions (-5,0,5), each carrying a Lantern model attached below it. Place the Duck floating on the water plane at (0,0,2). Physics system: implement simplified Euler integration with gravity, buoyancy (upward force when lantern is heated), drag, and wind. Each stork-lantern pair is a rigid body with combined mass. UI panel (HTML overlay) with: (1) sliders for lantern heat intensity per stork (affects buoyancy force 0-20N), (2) a global wind vector control with X and Z sliders (-10 to 10), (3) a mass adjustment slider per stork (0.5-5kg), (4) a button to release/attach lanterns from storks. The duck can be click-dragged across the water surface to set the target checkpoint. Interaction steps: user adjusts heat on stork 1, changes wind X, changes wind Z, adjusts mass of stork 2, releases lantern from stork 1, drags duck to new position, adjusts heat on stork 3, uses undo button to revert last parameter change, uses redo to reapply, adjusts mass of stork 1, then releases lantern from stork 3. Implement an undo/redo stack storing full parameter snapshots. Display a real-time HUD showing total kinetic energy, total potential energy, and total system energy to demonstrate conservation laws. When a lantern lands within 2 units of the duck, flash a green success indicator. Animate stork wing flapping using a sine-based rotation on wing bones if available, otherwise oscillate the whole model gently. All forces must be coupled so changing wind affects all airborne objects simultaneously.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  stork1Heat: number,         // heat intensity for stork 1 lantern (0-20N buoyancy force)
  stork2Heat: number,         // heat intensity for stork 2 lantern (0-20N buoyancy force)
  stork3Heat: number,         // heat intensity for stork 3 lantern (0-20N buoyancy force)
  windX: number,         // global wind force X component (-10 to 10)
  windZ: number,         // global wind force Z component (-10 to 10)
  stork1Mass: number,         // mass of stork 1 in kg (0.5-5)
  stork2Mass: number,         // mass of stork 2 in kg (0.5-5)
  stork3Mass: number,         // mass of stork 3 in kg (0.5-5)
  lanternsAttached: boolean,         // whether lanterns are attached to storks
  duckPositionX: number,         // duck X position on water surface
  duckPositionZ: number,         // duck Z position on water surface
  stork1Height: number,         // current Y position of stork 1
}
